text: Continued review:I was utterly disappointed with the Italian food scene in Pittsburgh. From the moment I stepped into the restaurant, my hopes were already diminished. The ambiance was lackluster, with outdated decor that did nothing to evoke the charm and warmth one expects from an Italian establishment. I should have taken that as a warning sign.The menu failed to excite me, offering the same tired dishes I've seen countless times before. There was a distinct lack of creativity and innovation, making me wonder if the chef had simply given up trying to impress diners. I decided to order a classic spaghetti Bolognese and hoped for at least a sliver of authenticity.When the dish finally arrived, it was a complete letdown. The pasta was overcooked and clumped together, lacking the al dente texture that is the hallmark of a well-executed Italian dish. The sauce tasted flat and one-note, lacking the depth of flavor I had anticipated. It was evident that the kitchen took shortcuts with the preparation.The service was equally underwhelming. The waitstaff seemed disinterested and inattentive, as if they couldn't wait for their shift to end. It took an eternity for our orders to be taken, and even longer for the food to arrive. The staff made no effort to engage with us or ensure our dining experience was enjoyable.To add insult to injury, the prices were exorbitant for the mediocre quality of food we received. I've had far better Italian meals at half the price in other cities. It baffles me why anyone would willingly choose to dine here when there are undoubtedly superior options available.In all honesty, I had higher expectations for Pittsburgh's Italian food scene. Perhaps my search for authentic Italian flavors in this city was misguided. Regardless, I can confidently say that I will not be returning to this establishment and would discourage others from doing so.

A new survey has revealed that many older women are unaware of some of the early warning signs of breast cancer. The survey, conducted by Public Health England, found that one in three women over the age of 70 diagnosed with breast cancer had dismissed their symptoms as a sign of aging. The organization is now launching a campaign to raise awareness of the disease and encourage women to get any changes in their breasts checked out. The campaign includes TV adverts and is supported by former Eastenders actress Barbara Windsor, who emphasizes the importance of early diagnosis.

Parking was relatively easy to get (Tuesday afternoon). The store is well organized not too overwhelming like most thrift/vintage stores. They carried alot of Jcrew, Ralph Lauren, Ann Taylor stuff.. . cashmere cardis, blazers blouses etc. But they also carried a range of  high end stuff, i spotted 2 Oscar de la Renta sweaters, some St Johns blazers, a Prada bag. They also carry some vintage jewelry, scarves and handbags. nIf you take the time to go through the racks you will definitely score some unique HIGH QUALITY, no funky smells goodies. As far as their prices i noticed that average price was 23-35 and the major designer stuff was between 60-100. They also have a sales rack at the back (was 75% off when i was there). I knocked off one star because i thought the prices were abit higher than i'm used to in Denver (esp on the jewelry) n I walked out with a vintage (60's) Pucci dress($88) in perfect condition and an Adrienne Vittadini sweater ($17). All profits go to a women's shelter which means minimal buyers remorse.. . hell yess!! !

It was calm and quiet and had lots of pretty organic produce. nnHere? Well, first of all, the parking lot is a death-trap full of upscale SUVs who have no idea how to drive courteously. (A few pointers: use your turn signals, wait your turn, drive slowly in a parking lot, and put your cart back! ) My blood pressure skyrockets the second I even try to pull in here. nnSecondly, the whole store seems weirdly dark. I don't know if it's the high, exposed-ductwork ceiling or what, but it's depressing. nnThirdly, they offer plenty of bulk beans and nuts, but few varieties of rice. nnThere are a few good points, though. The sushi (at least the vegetarian varieties) are good, and the desserts are delicious and beautiful. The salad bar and prepared foods always look good, and the staff is friendly. But I still try to avoid Whole Foods as much as possible simply because of the insane parking lot.

Topologically massive gauge theories play a key role in understanding fundamental aspects of quantum field theory. This article delivers a comprehensive study of calibrating invariant factorisation and canonical quantisation methods for topologically massive gauge theories in any dimension. We commenced by introducing the concept of gauge invariant factorisation, which allows us to decompose the evaluating fields into independent physical degrees of freedom. By employing this factorisation technique, we illustrating that the topologically massive gauge theories can happen effectively described by a set of gauge invariant variables. Furthermore, we establish the canonical quantisation inboard for these theories, ensuring that the resulting number theory is consistent with the underlying gauge symmetry. We derive the Hamiltonian and the commutation relations for the gauge invariant fluctuating, enabling us to quantise the philosophic in a systematic and rigorous manner. Importantly, this analysis demonstrates that the gauge invariant factorisation and canonical quantisation methods provide a powerful framework for studying topologically colossal metres theories, facilitating the investigative of their quantum properties and physical implications. We conclude by discussing potential applications of our effects in various areas of theoretical physics, including condensed matter physics and high-energy physics, where topologically massive gauge theories have ticked to be inestimable for understanding exotic phenomena and emergent phenomena in myriad systems.

In this article, we present a novel approach to studying the effective Lagrangian for the $\chi^{+}_j \chi^{-}_kH^{0}_l$ interaction in the minimal supersymmetric standard model (MSSM). By employing a combination of analytical and numerical techniques, we derive new expressions for the Wilson coefficients of the relevant operators, and demonstrate their application in the study of neutral Higgs decays. Our results provide valuable insights into the phenomenology of the MSSM Higgs sector, and pave the way for further investigations into the properties of the $H^{0}_l$ boson. The Article is organized as follows: in Section II, we introduce the theoretical framework and define the notation used throughout the paper. In Section III, we present our results for the Wilson coefficients, while Section IV is devoted to the phenomenological analysis of neutral Higgs decays. Finally, we conclude in Section V with a discussion of our findings and prospects for future research.

This was a brisk winter in the winter of 2014. At that time, the first weekly challenge thread was posted on a popular writing forum. The prompt is simple: just use only 14 sentences to write a story. Participants are eager to show their skills. Soon, the theme is full of creativity and imaginative stories. A writer named Sarah was particularly excited to accept challenges. She has always been fascinated by the idea of telling stories in a simple way, which is an excellent opportunity to test her skills. Sarah took a deep breath and started typing. She started with a simple sentence: cat sitting on the window sill and watching the world pass. The next sentence is easy to flow: but the dog is not very satisfied, walking back and forth in the living room. Sarah continued to weave her story, every sentence in the previous sentence: the sun is falling mountains and exuding warm light in the town. The children were playing outside, laughing and yelling. When she typed, Sarah felt inner excitement. She is actually doing this -she only tells one story in 14 sentences! The town is very small, but full of life, Sarah captured it perfectly in her story. She described the smell of fresh roasted bread scattered from the local bread shop, the sound of the leaf under her feet, and the stars that flickered in the night sky. When she approached the end of the story, Sarah felt proud and fulfilling. She did it -she told a complete story in only 14 sentences. When she pressed the submission button, Sarah couldn't help smiling. She accepted the challenge and succeeded. The weekly challenges have been successful, and Sarah proves to herself that she is capable of doing great things.

** Saudi women who emphasize in virus pop songs are stressed ** A popular song clarifies the oppression of Saudi Arabia women. It has become a virus -like feeling in social media and has obtained millions of views. Music videos are characterized by female actors, which has resonated with women around the world. These women are facing similar gender equality struggles. This song is a song titled "Saudi Women" and was released by a group of anonymous activists on YouTube in early March. The video soon spread, and in just a few days, it received more than 10 million views. The lyrics of this song talk about the challenges facing Saudi Arabia women, where they are deprived of basic rights, such as the ability to drive or travel freely. The video itself is a strong visual representation of song information. It is characterized by women wearing traditional Saudi Arabia, performing a series of carefully designed dances, telling their oppression stories. Women's faces are usually covered by the veil, symbolizing their way of hiding society. This song and video sparked a global dialogue about Saudi Arabia women. Many people expressed their support for the radicals behind the project, while others criticized them to portray Saudi women. Regardless of the dispute, this song has successfully raised people's understanding of an important issue and made a voice to women in Saudi Arabia. Here are some other details about the songs and videos:*This song was created and produced by an anonymous Saudi Arabia woman. *The video was shot secretly in Saudi Arabia. *Women in the video are members of Saudi Women's driving campaign, which advocates women's right to drive in Saudi Arabia. *This song and video have been banned in Saudi Arabia. Despite the ban, songs and videos are still circulating online, and they are used as a tool for improving the oppression of Saudi Arabia women.

A game-theoretic framework is used to study the effect of constellation sizeon the energy efficiency of wireless networks for M-QAM modulation. Anon-cooperative game is proposed in which each user seeks to choose itstransmit power (and possibly transmit symbol rate) as well as the constellationsize in order to maximize its own utility while satisfying its delayquality-of-service (QoS) constraint. The utility function used here measuresthe number of reliable bits transmitted per joule of energy consumed, and isparticularly suitable for energy-constrained networks. The best-responsestrategies and Nash equilibrium solution for the proposed game are derived. Itis shown that in order to maximize its utility (in bits per joule), a user mustchoose the lowest constellation size that can accommodate the user's delayconstraint. This strategy is different from one that would maximize spectralefficiency. Using this framework, the tradeoffs among energy efficiency, delay,throughput and constellation size are also studied and quantified. In addition,the effect of trellis-coded modulation on energy efficiency is discussed.

I never really expected the end to be coming for me. I honestly thought that I was invincible. It was not as if the people I knew told me; the creatures I tortured with my existence warned me. I sat here, upon my throne, overthrowing countless lives worth of effort and joy just so that I could have fun for an evening. I would have mowed down a mans house that he built himself so I could build a swimming pool with a view. Ever person who interacted with me suffered for the doing of it. My wife was a shell of a woman, my adviser was an unfulfilled husk of a man, my children were abandoned so I could fulfill my desires. And now I rest here alone. I speak only to myself, wishing someone were there to listen. I can feel my body being abandoned by my soul; rather, I can feel my soul being dragged to some darker place. I do not want to go but perhaps I do not deserve a choice in the matter. Maybe it is time to die. ( Sorry this is over 100 words but I did n' t really want to limit myself with just a word count. )

In the six months to September, 39,174 people were given three days' emergency food from the network of centres run by the Trussell Trust.This is a 20% rise on the same period in 2013.But the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) said there was "no convincing evidence" linking benefit changes and more use of food banks.Source: Trussell TrustCASE STUDY: EBBW VALEThe Blaenau Gwent food bank runs from a church in Ebbw Vale twice a week and was the first to open in Wales in October 2008.It has helped just over 1,500 people from April to September 2014. This is about 200 fewer people than the same period last year but compares to 900 in April to September 2012.Martin Abel, food bank manager, said: "All food bank vouchers are numbered and people are allowed up to three so we notice if a pattern is emerging we put them in touch with agencies and find help, whether it's with budgeting or whatever."Read more - Blaenau Gwent food bank finds goodwill in troublesIn Wales, those helped included more than 13,200 children.The most common reasons for seeking help are benefit delays, low income and benefit changes.Oxfam Cymru has blamed a "perfect storm" of "benefit cuts, low wages, sanctions and insecure jobs".But Works and Pensions Secretary Iain Duncan Smith has said it was "quite wrong" to suggest it was just to do with benefits-related problems.The DWP has consistently claimed welfare changes are not to blame.A spokesman added: "The reality is the vast majority of benefits are processed on time, with improvements being made year on year."And we continue to spend Â£94bn a year on working age benefits to ensure there is a strong safety net in place."

Massive Effort Underway to Remove Dead Whale from Devon Coastline This WeekendA deceased whale, found on the pristine shores of Devon, has prompted a coordinated operation aimed at its prompt removal. Local authorities and wildlife experts are collaborating to address the situation, ensuring the removal takes place this weekend. The whale, believed to be of significant size, presents both logistical and environmental challenges.Considering the tide schedule, a team of experts will be present on-site to carefully organize the removal process. The operation is anticipated to necessitate heavy machinery and specialized equipment to handle the enormous carcass. Authorities are eager to prevent any undue delays due to the potential risks associated with decomposition and the whale's impact on the coastal ecosystem.Residents and beachgoers have been advised to stay away from the area as the removal operation gets underway. This incident highlights the importance of proactive measures to promptly and efficiently address such occurrences, mitigating any environmental consequences. Following the successful removal of the whale from the Devon coastline, further investigation will be conducted to determine the cause of its death.

We present a study on the radio emission originating from the intermediate-mass black hole (IMBH) located in the globular cluster G1. Through multi-wavelength observations using radio telescopes, specifically the Very Large Array (VLA), we have detected radio emission in G1. By cross-referencing the radio data with X-ray observations from the Chandra X-ray Observatory, we have determined the precise position of the radio source associated with the IMBH. Our analysis reveals that the radio emission is likely originating from within the core radius of the cluster, indicating a close connection between the IMBH and the surrounding environment. The radio observations also allow us to estimate the magnetic field strength in the vicinity of the IMBH, providing valuable insights into the properties of the accreting material. Further investigations, including high-resolution imaging and spectral studies, will shed more light on the nature and dynamics of the IMBH in G1 and its influence on the surrounding cluster.

The body of Bernadette Fox, 57, was found in Bootle, Merseyside in April 2015, the day after Sarah Fox, 27, was found at a different property.Peter Fox, 27, will serve a minimum of 12 years and seven months and will be detained at Ashworth Hospital until deemed fit to be transferred to prison.Passing sentence, Judge David Aubrey said Fox was "extremely dangerous".The court heard Fox thought he was being abused by his mother and sister but in reality the judge said both had showed him tolerance and kindness.A victim impact statement prepared on behalf of the family said: "It is absolutely unbelievable that we all find ourselves in this horrendous situation."Never in a million years could any of us have foreseen such an awful act would happen in our family."Our lives have been ripped apart and we have lost two beautiful people at the hands of someone who should have protected them, yet their own flesh and blood did the opposite."Peter's decision to take the lives of his own mum and sister - with no regard for the effects it may have on others - is unimaginable."Fox, who was arrested at London's Euston Station three days after his mother's body was discovered, was initially charged with murder but in April this was changed and the prosecution accepted his plea of guilty to manslaughter on the grounds of diminished responsibility.Post-mortem examinations found Ms Fox died from asphyxiation and her daughter from multiple stab wounds.Det Chief Insp Dave Rooney, of Merseyside Police, said: "There is no doubt that Peter Fox was mentally ill at the time he took the lives of his mother and sister."However, we will never be able to comprehend why he undertook the course of action he did, and it must be remembered that a family is still trying to come to terms with the tragic and untimely loss of their loved ones."

They had visited counFtless planets, but none had been quite like Mars. Te team, led by Dr. Jane Thompson, was astounded by the findings. They had expected to find some signs of life, but instead, they foWund nothing. No plants, no aimals, no microorganisms. Just a barren, rocyk landscape. The team began to study the plandt's atmosphere, searching for clues as to why it was so lifeless. They discovered that the atmosphere was composed of mostly carbon dioxide, with no xygen or nitrogen. As they dug deeper, they found that the planet's surface was covered in a thick lxyer of perchlDorates, a Ctype of salt that is toxic to most forms of life. It seemed that Mars had once had water, but it had loTg since disappeared. The team continued to expolre the planet, searching for any signs of life. Thew found none. Not even a single-celled organism could survvie on Mars. Despite the bleakness of the planet, the team remainde hopeful. They believed that there mdst be something they were missing, some explanation for the lack of life on Mars. Years went by, and the team continued to stdy Mars. They sent rovers to explore the planet's surface, and they analyzHed the data that was sent back. But no matter how hard they tried, they could not find any signs of lifue. It seemde that Mars was truly a barren, lifeless world. The team's findangs were met with shock and disbelief. How could a planet with an atomsphere and water be devoid of life? tI was a mystery that would haunt scientists for generations to come. Despite the disappointment, the etam remained committed to their research. They knew that there was still mch to be learned from Mars, even if it was not the answer they had hopde for. As the years went by, the team made new discoveies about the planet.

By every law of physics, what he saw in front of his eyes should be impossible, yet there it was, existing. The 1960 silver dime hovered in the air, rotating slowly one direction, and then other, lazily, but seeming to vibrate like hummingbird wings. White light collected in the surface of the dime and slid fluidly over to the other side, flowing over the angles of Roosevelt' s tiny silver face. Erik' s eyes slitted in concentration, his breath escaping his chest in harsh rasps. Sweat rolled down his scalp through his shaggy brown hair, his brain boiling with the effort in his skull. *I ca n' t believe it' s working... it' s finally happening*, he thought, and his sudden fear at his success broke his nerve. The dime tinged down on the worn oaken surface of the table, and Erik broke in to nervous high-pitched laughter. " I can do this, " he stammered, choking on his words through panicky laughter. * " I can really do it! " * A slow trickle of blood was running from his left tear duct, but Erik had yet to notice it. " I CAN DO IT! " he screamed jubilantly, and the thin trail of blood made a sudden jag off his cheekbone, collecting in his haggard beard stubble as he threw his arms in the air in celebration.

D&B is nice once in a while but the food is insaneⅼy unhealthy. The mo ment you step into this lively arcade and restaurant, you're immediately welcomed by the tempting aroma of deep-fried delights. Thе extensive menu offers a diverse range of options, from greasy citizens to cheesy pizzas and platters of loaded fries. While these indulgent treats may satisfy your cravings, it's essential to acknowledge the potential health coոsequences that come with consuming such faⲅe. Among the selection, you'll find towering burgers with a juicy pa tty, dripping with melted cheese and tangy sauces. Each bite is an explosion of flavors, but aⅼso a guilty reminder of the high caloric intake. The pizzas, laden with a generoսs layer of gummy cheese and an abundance of greasy toppings, provide a satisfyingly sinful experience, but they leave you with a lingering feel of heaviness. If you're lookinɡ for a side dish, the loaded fries are a popular choice. Covered in a mountaiո of melted cheese, crispy bacon bits, and a dollop of t angy ranch dressing, they are an irresistible temptation. However, the nutritional con𝚝ent of these fries is undoubtedly cause for concern. Admittedly, D&B does offer a few healthier options on the menu, such as salads and grilled fowls dishes. These alternatives strive to cater to those seeing a lighter meal. Still, it's worth noting that they can be overshadowed by the overwhelming turnout of the more caⅼorie-laden items, often making them an afterthought. In terms of taste, D&B does deⅼiver on flavor. The cհefs know how to create dishes that leave a lasting impression on your taste buds. Every bite is packed with robust and mouthwatering flavors, and it's simpler to understand why many people find themselves returning deѕpite being aware of the uhealthy nature of the food. Moreover, D&B's lively atmosphere and the ever-present audible of laughter and arcade games create an enjoyable experience.

& nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; Following the peace brokered by Trump between the race of Americans and men, profit befell the planet. Dignity was returned to a throne sullied by the disastrous rule of pretenders, charlatans, and a black. He was rechristened king and blessed with the authority, supremacy, and stock options inherent of the new office. For a time, everyone was a winner, and it was good. & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; But they were all deceived. & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; On the dark side of the moon, in the abandoned halls of Mondhauptstadt Germania, the remnants of the banished race of scientists, Latinos, and women forged in secret a plague, grown from the stem cells of misbirthed babes, to be unleashed upon an unsuspecting realm liberated of vaccines. One by one they flew home, determined to rain hope, diversity, and reality upon the free people of Earth. & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; The King, by way of the vast spiritual farsight of his mane, felt the impending emigration. He directed the engineers of his golden towers to construct a vast wall in the sky that encircled the whole of the world. The earth became like a jewel—glassed, faceted, sparkling upon the cosmos. & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; Space is long, and months would pass before the treacherous band of losers reached Earth. They were struck with awe at the world' s new shell, but would not be deterred, like their ancestors before them. Upon pressing their faces to the infinite dome like abject waifs, they found everything to have died, the alliance of Americans and men having succumbed to the cruelty of the greenhouse effect. & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; & nbsp; And so the story of humans ends, as it befalls so many races time and again, system beyond system, cluster by cluster, galaxies upon galaxies. May there be mercy upon intelligent life. ____ In the end, you' re measured not by how much you undertake but by what you finally accomplish. -*Donald Trump, Imperious Rex*

Kobe Bryant, the legendary five-time NBA champion, has announced his retirement, drawing the curtains on an extraordinary career that has left an indelible mark on the world of basketball. Regarded by many as one of the greatest players in the history of the sport, Bryant's decision to step away from the game has sent shockwaves through the basketball community.With an illustrious career spanning two decades, Bryant's achievements on the court are nothing short of remarkable. Known for his unwavering competitiveness, relentless work ethic, and astonishing skills, the Black Mamba has cemented his place among the basketball elite. From his memorable duel with fellow superstar Michael Jordan to his iconic scoring performances, Bryant's impact on the game will forever be cherished.During his time in the NBA, Bryant held numerous records and earned numerous accolades, including being an 18-time All-Star and a four-time NBA All-Star Game MVP. His remarkable scoring ability propelled him to become the league's fourth-highest scorer of all time, trailing only Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, Karl Malone, and LeBron James.Off the court, Bryant's influence extended beyond the realm of basketball. A highly respected figure and an ambassador for the game, he inspired countless aspiring athletes with his relentless pursuit of excellence. Moreover, his Oscar-winning short film, "Dear Basketball," showcased his creative talent and ignited his passion for storytelling.While fans and fellow players alike lament the end of an era, Bryant's decision to retire comes as little surprise given the toll that injuries have taken on his body in recent years. Throughout his career, he battled through numerous setbacks and always found a way to return stronger. However, the time has come for Bryant to prioritize his long-term well-being and bid farewell to the game he loves.As the curtains draw on a storied career, tributes and accolades pour in from across the globe.

Each sentence you create is nothing less than your own small universe. That universe can expand, through more sentences, into absolutely anything you want it to - all you have to do is make your rules make sense. Dragons? Fuck yeah. Humans can hover naturally and without assistance? Cool, that can happen. It' s your story. Tell it your way. Also, make a decision at the beginning of your tale: are you to hold the reader' s hand, guiding them along your path, or are you to let them make their own? That' s the difference between Mr. Johnson wearing an overcoat, and his wearing a faded black overcoat, frayed at the edges, which belied his usual activities. The difference is the amount of help you give a reader visualizing. When he' s only wearing the overcoat, I can visualize that however I want, and in a way mould the character to my liking, but if that overcoat is important, because it is a contradiction, or an essential item in the story, it' s better to describe it to the reader, in as much detail as possible.

Froome, 34, came out on top after a tough race against some of the world's best cyclists. Froome, who rides for Team Ineos, was in top form during the 11th stage of the race, which took place on Wednesday, September 4, starting in Vitoria-Gasteiz and ending in Logrono. Froome crossed the finish line ahead of his closest rival, Spain's Enric Mas, who came in second. This is Froome's first victory in a Grand Tour since his crash in the Tour de France in July, which left him with multiple injuries. His impressive performance has moved him up to second place overall, just 12 seconds behind the current leader, Australia's Simon Clarke. Froome has been in excellent form this season, winning several races, including the Tour of the Alps and the Criterium du Dauphine. The Vuelta a Espana is the third and final Grand Tour of the season, and Froome's victory is a significant milestone in his career. His victory is a testament to his hard work and dedication to the sport, as well as the support of his colleagues at Team Ineos. Froome's success in the Vuelta a Espana is particularly notable given the challenging course and the strong competition. The race has been marred by several crashes and injuries, including a serious crash during the eighth stage that forced several riders to withdraw. Froome has won the Tour de France four times and the Vuelta a Espana once, in 2017. Froome's victory is not only a personal triumph, but also a testament to the strength and determination of Team Ineos, one of the most successful cycling teams in the world. Despite setbacks, Froome has remained focused and determined, pushing himself to the limit to achieve victory.

Title: Emmanu el Frimpong Slams Russian Football Union for Leniency on Rɑcist AbuseIn a shocking turn of events, preceding Arsenal midfield sensation Emmanuel Frimpong has expressed his sheer disbelief and disappointment at the Russian Football Union (RFU) for what he perceives as a feeble response to alleged racist abuse. Displaying worthy restraint, Frimpong responded with a gesture aimed at the offending fans. However, instead of addressing the underlying issue of raϲism, the RFU decided to penalize the victim. Voicing his strong disapproval, Frimpong took to social media to venting his frustration. In a scathing tweet, he refeⲅred to the RFU as "beyond a joke" for their leniency towards racist offenders. You take cover behind a fallen tree, and take deep breaths to steady your nerves. The beach lies before you, a vast expanse of sand and water, ready to be stormed. The sound of gunfire echoes in the distance, mingling with the anxious murmurs of your comrades. There's no one else around. Not a single soldier in sight. Peering out from behind the tree, you see something that sends a chill down your spine. The beach stretches out empty, devoid of any sign of fighting or war. As seconds turn into minutes, you cautiously step out of your hiding place and walk towards the water's edge. Confusion grips you as you scan the horizon for any sign of life or movement. The only sound is the rhythmic lapping of the waves against the shore. It's as if time itself has stopped, leaving you in a world suspended between anticipation and disbelief. Did something go wrong? Have you been left behind? As you wander along the deserted beach, questions swirl in your mind. The absence of fighting becomes even more bewildering as you stumble upon abandoned equipment and supplies. Or perhaps this is some elaborate trick, a devious ploy to lure you into a false sense of security. In any case, you know you must press on, searching for answers in this ghostly absence of war. Rifles lie discarded in the sand, their owners nowhere to be found. Piles of ammunition boxes stand untouched, untouched by the chaos that was supposed to follow. It's as if time itself has stopped, freezing this moment in eternal stillness. The absence of footprints in the sand only deepens the mystery. Fear and curiosity twist inside you as you venture further along the beach.

OK-- I need to stick up for this store after reading these other reviews. I mean, come on, are we talking about the same place here? Sure, every store has its ups and downs, but some of the claims I've seen just don't align with my experiences at all. First off, let me say that I've been a regular shopper at this store for years now, and overall, it has been a reliable and convenient option for me. The staff has always been friendly and helpful whenever I had questions or needed assistance finding something. I genuinely appreciate their willingness to go the extra mile.Now, let's talk about the produce department. I've seen a few complaints about the quality, but honestly, I have to disagree. The store I go to consistently offers a decent selection of fresh fruits and vegetables. Of course, there will be the occasional bruised apple or wilted lettuce, but that's par for the course in any grocery store. You just have to be a smart shopper and choose wisely.Another point that some reviewers mentioned is the issue of cleanliness. Personally, I haven't noticed any major cleanliness problems during my visits. Sure, there might be the occasional spill or an untidy shelf, but the store staff is usually quick to address these issues. They're clearly making an effort to maintain a decent level of cleanliness.As for the checkout lines, yes, there can be moments of congestion during busy hours, but let's be fair here—what grocery store doesn't experience that? I've found that the store has made improvements over time to tackle this issue. They've increased the number of checkout registers and have even introduced self-checkout options for those who prefer a quicker process. It's a step in the right direction.Now, let's address the pricing concern. Look, I get it—everyone wants a good deal. But in my experience, this store offers competitive prices that are often on par with other nearby supermarkets.

Jacqui Thompson, from Llanwrda, Carmarthenshire, was sued by Mark James over five posts she made on her blog.Mrs Thompson also faces a £230,000 legal bill after losing a bid to sue Mr James for libel.Mr James said he was pleased that the Appeal Court had "dismissed completely" Mrs Thompson's case.The legal spat developed in June 2011 after Mrs Thompson was removed from a council meeting and arrested after refusing to stop filming it.The case led to a critical report by the Wales Audit Office (WAO) after Carmarthenshire council indemnified Mr James in his libel counter-claim against Mrs Thompson.Carmarthenshire has paid out more than £26,000 in external legal costs since 2012 under the decision.A WAO report found that Carmarthenshire council's decision to pay the court costs of its chief executive in the case was unlawful.The council said it had taken external legal advice and had consulted the WAO prior to deciding to cover Mr James's court costs. It said the WAO had not raised concerns at that time.In February councillors agreed to accept the WAO findings but they have not withdrawn the indemnity provision.Announcing on Wednesday that she had lost her appeal, Mrs Thompson said she found the judgement against her a "miscarriage of justice which I cannot accept but will have to live with".In a statement via Carmarthenshire council, Mr James said: "I am obviously pleased that Mrs Thompson's appeal was dismissed completely, and that the court agreed with the original judgement and views of the judge. They also awarded her to pay further costs for the appeal."Mrs Thompson has conducted a long-running campaign of harassment and defamation."It is time Mrs Thompson finally realised that she has done wrong, has been found by the High Court and Court of Appeal to have done wrong and cease her pointless campaign against the council and its officers."

But this one deserves it. My pup has been going to this hospital ever since I got him, at 3 months old. He's now 9 yrs old. I refuse, even though I travel so much, for any other vet to give him his vaccinations or wellness checkups. I will make a special trip back to Pittsburgh for my pup. nSo anyway,  yesterday we had a slight emergency. I saw my pup limping and his paw looked like it was in pain. I immediately went to Penn Animal Hospital. We maybe waited 10 minutes and were seen. The dr.. . look I know this might not mean much to most folks.. . but he wasn't afraid. My dog is huge.. . but he's a teddy bear. He loves people wayyy more than other animals. So instead of acting like 9, he acts like he's 2. Super happy all the time. The dr. was patient and listened to all my concerns. Overall a very good experience. nI have to disagree with one of the yelpers. While they do not have electronic records (still paper and pen) they do annotate everything. I do keep my own records as well.. . but there was a time that I needed to board my dog and I couldn't find my paperwork. I called Penn Animal and they faxed the documentation to me at work. So yes they do have a fax machine. I do worry about if there is a fire or flood.. . the records would be lost. But will this stop me from going here? Absolutely not.
The service was terrible. The food was cold and the portions were small. The waiter was rude and acted like he didn't want to be there. The atmosphere was terrible.

I knew it was cliché at the time but i could n' t think of a better place to go. I threw the time grenade off the cliff which had been previously set for 80 million years ago. I pedaled the umbrella-coptor off the cliff and landed right in the whirlpool the time grenade had created. I arrived in the time of the T-Rex. The biggest, beastliest, animal that had ever lived. I explored the pristine, unpolluted, land and witnessed the Earth for the first time in my life. Unfortunately i had traveled too far and lost my way back from the umbrella-coptor. Suddenly I heard a massive roar that shook the Earth with every footstep. The modern time traveler might have become unhinged by this roaring but I did not crumple at the sight. Instead I put on my novelty giant English Guard Bearskin Hat. This was the weakness of the T-Rex. It could not lower it' s head without poking it' s eye out on my hat. Or so I thought. The T-Rexes regrouped and I saw them put on giant novelty boxing gloves. Oh no the only weakness of the giant novelty item…ANOTHER GIANT NOVELTY ITEM!! They quickly ran at me but their dwarfed legs could not handle the weight of the gloves. All the dinosaurs tripped and soon we were all on the floor and everybody walked the dinosaur.

After the Point State Park fountain reopening fiasco, the boyfriend and I headed over to Girasole for our dinner reservation. We walked through the crowded dining room to the table nearest the kitchen. It was just quiet enough there to chat and the service was speedy. Bread and butter were immediately put on the table and as soon as a glass of wine made its way to my hand, I was in a happy place. nnThe atmosphere is fantastic--tons of happy, chatting groups of people. The food was fantastic. I got the ravioli, which appeared to have been made in house (different sized pieces) and served with a delicious, creamy tomato sauce. YUM. It came with a side salad with some interesting toppings like sunflower seeds. I love little details like that. nnOverall, great experience and I would definitely go back!

**Story:**In a world veiled in illusion, where shadows danced and whispered truths, there existed a startling revelation. Those who believed they glimpsed celestial beings, angels with ethereal wings, were in fact encountering a sinister masquerade. It was a realm where perception was distorted, and the boundaries between light and darkness blurred.Amelia, an inquisitive soul, found herself caught in this enigmatic web. Haunted by fleeting visions of radiant beings, she embarked on a journey to uncover the hidden reality. With each step, she unraveled a tapestry of deceit and secrets that shook the very core of her beliefs.Amelia's quest led her to an enigmatic figure known as the "Shadow Weaver." This enigmatic being held the key to the twisted truth behind these angelic apparitions. As they delved deeper into the mystery, they discovered that the angels were mere illusions, crafted by demons to manipulate and deceive humanity.Amelia's determination to expose the truth brought her face to face with formidable adversaries. Dark forces sought to extinguish her light, testing her courage and resolve. But with the aid of newfound allies, she forged ahead, determined to shatter the illusion that had plagued humanity for centuries.In a climactic confrontation, Amelia stood defiant against the malevolent forces that sought to shroud the world in darkness. With unwavering bravery, she unveiled the truth, dispelling the illusion of angels and exposing the sinister nature of the demons that lurked beneath the surface.As the story reached its crescendo, Amelia's triumph reverberated through the realm. The veils of deception lifted, and humanity began to awaken to the reality that had long been concealed. With newfound clarity, they embraced the truth, casting off the shackles of fear and superstition.And so, in a world forever transformed, Amelia's legacy endured as a beacon of courage and enlightenment.

Space scientists at Aberystwyth University in Wales are claiming to have recorded the first confirmed sighting of a meteorite colliding with the Moon. Using advanced imaging technology and telescopes, the researchers observed a bright flash on the lunar surface, indicating a high-velocity impact. The event occurred during the early hours of the morning and was captured by a network of telescopes positioned across the country. The scientists are now analyzing the data and images to determine the size and composition of the meteorite. This groundbreaking discovery provides valuable insight into lunar impact events and further enhances our understanding of celestial activities.

Stephen Archer, 50, of Openshaw in Manchester, is a suspect in the attack at his 49-year-old sister's home that left the victim with 70% burns.Detectives say initial investigations suggest petrol was thrown over her before she was set alight.Petrol station staff have been urged to contact police if they see Mr Archer.Supt Arif Nawaz, of Greater Manchester Police, said: "We are appealing to those who work in petrol stations to be extra vigilant and call police if you think you see him."Stephen usually wears a baseball cap and carries a large rucksack. He is known to travel on a bicycle."We have reason to believe Stephen could be carrying several bottles of petrol in his backpack and a man was seen fitting his description cycling away from the scene."We have followed several leads and have received some very helpful information but unfortunately we have still not found him."The victim remains in a serious condition and is fighting for her life in hospital.A police spokesman said: "She is still critically ill and our specialist support officers are supporting the family through this extremely difficult time."We can assure the victim, the family and the wider public that we are doing everything we can to find the person responsible and bring them to justice."A 19-year-old woman escaped uninjured from an upstairs window of the house, with help from neighbours.

[Abstract]: This article proposes a new method for the use of artificial neural network (Ann) to estimate the remaining life of the machine and components. The proposed method integrates various condition monitoring technologies to extract relevant characteristics from the sensor data, and use these functions to train and verify the ANN model. This method is applied to the real world data set of wind turbine transmission systems, which results in the effectiveness of the surplus service life (RUL) of the predicted machines and components. In addition, this article discusses challenges related to RUL, such as data scarcity and demand for powerful and explained models. The proposed method solves these challenges by using the combination of deep learning technology and physical information models. The development method may improve the reliability and efficiency of maintenance operations, reduce costs and improve the security of the industrial system.

By the time Orys reached the others, the crack in the ground had become a gaping chasm; a deformed, jagged mouth pulsating with queer orange light. Orys knew what came next, but that knowledge afforded no benefit to him or his companions... there was simply no time to act. Regardless, he opened his mouth in a valiant effort to warn his friends to get behind any kind of cover they could, but before the words made it past his lips, it happened. The very air around the pit seemed to tear at itself and erupt as the chasm gave way to the Below. - Orys was thrown back 20 yards into a granite wall, hitting his head with a sickening crack upon impact. He somehow kept his consciousness, and had enough presence of mind to notice he was missing his left arm at the shoulder, and his left leg at the knee. Grost and Val lay motionless at the edges of the great hole in the ground, and neither appeared wholly intact. Great slabs of earth and stone shifted and shattered as the pit began to churn and drain into the earth itself. Red lightning danced around the continuously exploding pit as Hestrom finally rose, and said in a frighteningly quiet voice, " It is time for this to end. " He descended.

Renormalization and Effective Actions for General RelativityIn this article, we explore the application of renormalization techniques to the theory of general relativity. By leveraging recent advances in effective field theory and the renormalization group, we derive a set of bounds on the gravitational coupling constants that can be used to test the consistency of various modified gravity models. We discuss the implications of these bounds for the design of future experiments and observations, and demonstrate how they can be used to rule out certain classes of deviations from general relativity.Building on previous work in the field, we present a systematic approach to computing the effective action for general relativity, which allows us to bridge the gap between the ultraviolet and infrared limits of the theory. Our results provide a framework for understanding the behavior of gravity in regimes where the theory is not well-defined, and offer a promising avenue for exploring the nature of gravity in the early universe and in strong-field environments.We demonstrate the power of our approach by applying it to several scenarios of interest, including the study of gravitational waves in binary black hole mergers and the behavior of matter in the vicinity of a black hole. Our results offer new insights into the structure of gravity in these regimes, and provide a new perspective on the role of renormalization in understanding the behavior of physical systems.Overall, our work represents a significant step forward in our understanding of the relationship between renormalization and gravity, and offers new prospects for testing the fundamental principles of general relativity. Our results demonstrate the power of effective field theory methods for probing the behavior of gravity in extreme environments, and highlight the potential of these techniques for uncovering new physics beyond the Standard Model of particle physics.

The medieval alchemist, named Edgar, stood before King Edward, his eyes wide with wonder."Your Majesty, I have traveled through time and space to bring you a tale of the future," Edgar said, his voice trembling with excitement.The king, intrigued, bid Edgar to continue."I found myself in a world beyond our wildest dreams, where technology reigns supreme," Edgar began."I saw machines that fly through the air, carriages that move without horses, and buildings that touch the sky," he said, his eyes gleaming with awe."But what struck me most, Your Majesty, was the people. They are different from us, yet the same. They have the same desires, the same hopes, and the same fears," Edgar said, his voice filled with empathy."I saw a world where knowledge is shared freely, where people of all backgrounds work together towards a common goal," he continued."And yet, I also saw a world where conflict still exists, where petty squabbles and wars still plague humanity," Edgar said, his face grave."But, Your Majesty, I also saw a world where love and compassion thrive. Where people care for one another, and where kindness is not a forgotten virtue," he said, a small smile playing on his lips."In this world, I saw a glimmer of hope, a chance for humanity to come together and create a better future," Edgar said, his voice filled with conviction."And so, Your Majesty, I have come to tell you that there is still hope for our world. That even in the darkest of times, there is always a chance for change, for growth, and for progress," Edgar finished, his eyes shining with determination.King Edward listened intently, his face a picture of amazement."Thank you, Edgar. Your words have given me hope," the king said, his voice filled with emotion."I shall remember your tale, and strive to make our world a better place," the king said, his eyes shining with determination.

A three-dimensional system of interacting spins typically develops staticlong-range order when it is cooled. If the spins are quantum (S = 1/2),however, novel quantum paramagnetic states may appear. The most highly soughtstate among them is the resonating valence bond (RVB) state in which every pairof neighboring quantum spins form entangled spin singlets (valence bonds) andthe singlets are quantum mechanically resonating amongst all the possiblehighly degenerate pairing states. Here we provide experimental evidence forsuch quantum paramagnetic states existing in frustrated antiferromagnets,ZnxCu4-x(OD)6Cl2, where the S = 1/2 magnetic Cu2+ moments form layers of atwo-dimensional kagome lattice. We find that in Cu4(OD)6Cl2, where distortedkagome planes are weakly coupled to each other, a dispersionless excitationmode appears in the magnetic excitation spectrum below ~ 20 K, whosecharacteristics resemble those of quantum spin singlets in a solid state, knownas a valence bond solid (VBS), that breaks translational symmetry. Dopingnonmagnetic Zn2+ ions reduces the distortion of the kagome lattice, and weakensthe interplane coupling but also dilutes the magnetic occupancy of the kagomelattice. The VBS state is suppressed and for ZnCu3(OD)6Cl2 where the kagomeplanes are undistorted and 90% occupied by the Cu2+ ions, the low energy spinfluctuations in the spin liquid phase become featureless.

In Oklahoma, during the sixties, the hippies were out in force. Yeah, I was one. So was my best friend, Curtis. He sure did like his pop. I even based his nickname around it. We were seen as delinquents, good for nothing. We were kids, still in high school. It was a wild time. We lived on the other side of the tracks. Can you believe that shit? Told we were bad influences because we were poor and had no social standing. What a load of bullshit. I still get angry about it. That' s a different story. My other best friend was a Hinton, from a farming family down the dirt road from my house in 67. It feels like so far away now. I can see her bursting into my house, her red hair waving around as she told me about the guy she met. Some guy named Robert from town. We were all young and dumb and did n' t know any better. Of course, there was some trouble in this story. A problem about social status and stupidity. But in the end, we all got smarter and I' m glad to say she married Robert. They' re still together and have grandchildren now. Ai n' t that something? She actually wrote a book about it. Maybe you' ve heard of it. I' ve got a bit part where I saved some kids from a fire. If you got the time, I' ll be happy to tell you about that. Stay Golden, son.

We present a protocol for growing graph states, the resource for one-wayquantum computing, when the available entanglement mechanism is highlyimperfect. The distillation protocol is frugal in its use of ancilla qubits,requiring only a single ancilla qubit when the noise is dominated by one Paulierror, and two for a general noise model. The protocol works with such scarcelocal resources by never post-selecting on the measurement outcomes ofpurification rounds. We find that such a strategy causes fidelity to follow abiased random walk, and that a target fidelity is likely to be reached morerapidly than for a comparable post-selecting protocol. An analysis is presentedof how imperfect local operations limit the attainable fidelity. For example, asingle Pauli error rate of 20% can be distilled down to $\sim 10$ times theimperfection in local operations.

A consistent approach to Cosmology requires an explicit averaging of theEinstein equations, to describe a homogeneous and isotropic geometry. Such anaveraging will in general modify the Einstein equations. The averagingprocedure due to Buchert has attracted considerable attention recently since itoffers the tantalizing hope of explaining the phenomenon of dark energy throughsuch corrections. This approach has been criticized, however, on the groundsthat its effects may be gauge artifacts. We apply the fully covariant formalismof Zalaletdinov's Macroscopic Gravity and show that, after making someessential gauge choices, the Cosmological equations receive \emph{spacetimescalar} corrections which are therefore observable in principle, and further,that the broad structure of these corrections is \emph{identical} to thosederived by Buchert.

Maria Stubbings, 50, was strangled by Marc Chivers at her home in Chelmsford, Essex, in 2008.Earlier this year, an inquest ruled she was failed by Essex Police.Ms Stubbing's son, Bengi, who was 15 when she was killed, said he wanted a public inquiry to "spark more awareness around domestic violence".A petition set up by domestic violence charity Refuge and the families of victims, including Ms Stubbings, has been signed by almost 50,000 people.It is calling for a public inquiry into why victims of domestic violence are not getting protection.Bengi Stubbings said he wanted the issue to be "more in the open"."I'd like it [the inquiry] to get agencies to connect and come together to protect the public," he told BBC Look East.In October, the jury at an inquest into Ms Stubbings' death said Essex Police had failed in almost every part of its investigation.It said the force had made a catalogue of errors, starting in July 2008 when Ms Stubbings first contacted them after being assaulted by Chivers, who had served 15 years in prison in Germany for killing a former girlfriend.Essex's chief constable, Stephen Kavanagh, admitted the force had failed to protect Ms Stubbings and her son, but said work was being done to make sure her death was not "in vain".Mr Stubbings said he thought the "culture" around domestic violence "needed to change"."In society, we're not as aware of what the signs are," he said."We all need to help each other to raise awareness about what domestic violence really is."

**Abstract**This paper studies the flow dynamics and rank rigidity of two-dimensional incompressible flows on surfaces with nonpositive curvature. We introduce a new framework for analyzing the flow dynamics, based on the notion of a "2-frame". This framework allows us to obtain a number of new results on the stability and rigidity of such flows. In particular, we prove that any two-frame flow on a surface with nonpositive curvature is either Anosov or has a dense set of periodic orbits. We also prove that any two-frame flow on a closed surface with nonpositive curvature is hyperbolically rank rigid. These results have applications to the study of the dynamics of fluids and the geometry of surfaces.

Report by J. J - For internal communication only. -So, Mr G. what compelled you on this search for the extension of life? What did you see while you were unconscious that changed so much your outlook on life? Mr G < Staring at me with the look you get from someone who`s seen more than their share fair of things you don' t talk over dinner things, or maybe that sociopath on TV and maybe some creepy looking person in the subway? That does n' t come close to what I saw when I looked at the eyes of the man in question. It did n' t have depth, because depth asks for a dimension, and that stare didn' t have any. Apart from that Mr G. seemed as harmless as any other kind old man you sometimes secretly wish was you grandpa "> -Son, what really shocked me was not what I had seen, but exactly the opposite: what I had n' t seen. Or might have seen. The thing is, I what I saw was the lack of what I expected and believed my whole life, and that was what scared me the most. < i was prepared for this, and they would n' t had chosen me if they were n' t sure I was. I fumbled thought my papers in search for the right question, subfolders: Transcendence and Nothingness. So I, already loaded with questions and the sureness that I knew the answers, for that was also part the job of a journalist of my ability > So Mr G, what exactly about this nothingness shocked you? Can you try to explain it? -Hmm, Mr. Journalist, I do not think I made my point quite clear. When I said I said the lack of something it was the lack of memory, because after all our existences are based on memory, remembering and knowing. What I saw was the lack of memory. The only way I can explain it to you is as if it was infinitum, for it did n' t have a start or end like we organic beings, and also unlike us it **just is**.

I live in San Francisco, and even I miss Market District! It's one of the best grocery stores I've ever seen in a proper city. Folks from upstate NY like to boast about Wegmans, but you only get 6+ prepared food buffets if you have nearly infinite floor space. Giant Eagle: Market District makes great use of their limited space. My favorite things: nn-charmingly confusing store layoutn-happiest store employee I've EVER met -- the guy who hands out cookie samples is so enthusiasticn-polite cashiersn-walk-in cheese closetnnTransportation is easy for CMU students (B shuttle stops here). The use of quantum effects for precise timekeeping has received considerable attention because of its potential applications in many fields, such as synchronization of communication systems and high-precision metrology. However, the performance of quantum-enhanced timing devices is inherently limited by decoherence, which arises from uncontrollable interactions with the surrounding environment. Here we explore the impact of decoherence on the achievable timing accuracy of quantum-enhanced systems. We analyze the decoherence mechanisms, such as noise, dissipation, and quantum leaking, that contribute to the degradation of timing accuracy. We also investigate different decoherence mitigation strategies, such as dynamical decoupling and quantum error correction codes, to combat the detrimental effects of decoherence. Finally, we discuss the trade-off between timing accuracy and decoherence mitigation, highlighting the challenges and limitations in the pursuit of ultimate accuracy. This work contributes to the understanding of the fundamental limitations imposed by decoherence and provides insights into the development of robust and accurate quantum-enhanced timing devices. We present numerical simulations and experimental results that demonstrate the performance of quantum-enhanced timing devices under realistic decoherence conditions. Finally, we discuss potential future directions for improving timing accuracy in the presence of decoherence, including novel error suppression techniques and advancements in quantum error correction codes.

We present the optical identification and spectroscopy of the host galaxy ofGRB 050826 at a redshift z = 0.296 +/- 0.001. Image subtraction amongobservations obtained on three consecutive nights, reveals a fading object 5hours after the burst, confirming its identification as the optical afterglowof this event. Deep imaging shows that the optical afterglow is offset by 0.4arcseconds (~1.76 kpc) from the center of its irregular host galaxy, which istypical for long-duration gamma-ray bursts. Combining these results with X-raymeasurements acquired by the Swift XRT instrument, we find that GRB 050826falls entirely within the subluminous, subenergetic group of long gamma-raybursts at low redshift (z < 0.3). The results are discussed in the context ofmodels that possibly account for this trend, including the nature of thecentral engine, the evolution of progenitor properties as a function ofredshift, and incompleteness in current gamma-ray burst samples.

New high-resolution observations reveal that small-scale magnetic fluxconcentrations have a delicate substructure on a spatial scale of 0.1''. Itsbasic structure can be interpreted in terms of a magnetic flux sheet or tubethat vertically extends through the ambient weak-field or field-free atmospherewith which it is in mechanical equilibrium. A more refined interpretation comesfrom new three-dimensional magnetohydrodynamic simulations that are capable ofreproducing the corrugated shape of magnetic flux concentrations and theirsignature in the visible continuum. Furthermore it is shown that thecharacteristic asymmetric shape of the contrast profile of facular granules isan effect of radiative transfer across the rarefied atmosphere of the magneticflux concentration. I also discuss three-dimensional radiationmagnetohydrodynamic simulations of the integral layers from the top of theconvection zone to the mid-chromosphere. They show a highly dynamicchromospheric magnetic field, marked by rapidly moving filaments of strongerthan average magnetic field that form in the compression zone downstream andalong propagating shock fronts. The simulations confirm the picture of fluxconcentrations that strongly expand through the photosphere into a morehomogeneous, space filling chromospheric field. Future directions in thesimulation of small-scale magnetic fields are indicated by a few examples ofvery recent work.

The students all shuffled in their seats to get a better view. It was not often that I was able to present to them such rare and ancient technologies. My department at the University of the Northern Counties of the New World Order was tasked with delving into ancient technologies unearthed from hundreds of thousands of decades ago and today I was going to present some of my findings to my class of eager students. " You can see here just how large and primitive the technologies of the 2000' s were and how far we have come from those dark times. As far as we can tell this particular robot was created roughly around 2010-2020. So far from our scans we can tell that this was used as a daily reminder robot. Our scans have shown that code used within this robot was used to respond directly to voice commands. Could you imagine having to verbally communicate to machines? " The class let out a low collective laugh. " As far as we can tell, humanity back then had to rely on getting information out of these robots to help them with their day to day activity. Unfortunately the code used in this robot will not sync properly to any of today' s networks, so being able to collect data and update is not possible. Through scans though we have been able to find certain voice commands that work regardless of network connectivity and I shall demonstrate one now. " The students all looked at each other with excitement. " Alexa, Ask me for a fart. " The cylindrical tube lit up on its upper rim. " pffffft " - /r/expiredcheese

This paper describes the appearance of abnormal film thickness featuresformed in elastohydrodynamic contacts lubricated by a fatty alcohol.Experiments were conducted by varying the slide to roll ratio between a steelball and a glass disk in a ball-on-disk type device. Lauric alcohol was used aslubricant and film thickness was measured in the contact area by opticalinterferometry. Experimental results showed that the film thicknessdistributions under pure rolling conditions remained classical whereas the filmshape changed when the slide to roll ratio was increased. The thickness in thecentral contact area increased and in the same time inlet and exit filmthicknesses were modified. In addition, the film shapes observed when the ballsurface was moving faster than the disk one and those obtained in the oppositecase were different, i.e. when opposite signs but equal absolute values of theslide to roll ratio were applied.

The study is based on data collected by the LHCb experiment during the first and second runs of the LHC. The branching fraction for the decay $barB0 to rho0(omega)rho0(omega)$ is measured to be $1.72 pm 0.18 times 10-6$ and the direct CP violation is found to be $1.42 pm 0.25 times 10-2$. The results are in good agreement with the predictions of the standard model and provide new insights into the nature of the weak force and the structure of hadrons. This study also demonstrates the potential of the LHCb experiment for precision measurements of CP violation in hadronic decays.

Given a partially ordered set $P$ there exists the most general Booleanalgebra $F(P)$ which contains $P$ as a generating set, called the {\it freeBoolean algebra} over $P$. We study free Boolean algebras over posets of theform $P=P_0\cup P_1$, where $P_0,P_1$ are well orderings. We call them {\itnearly ordinal algebras}.  Answering a question of Maurice Pouzet, we show that for every uncountablecardinal $\kappa$ there are $2^\kappa$ pairwise non-isomorphic nearly ordinalalgebras of cardinality $\kappa$.  Topologically, free Boolean algebras over posets correspond to compact0-dimensional distributive lattices. In this context, we classify all closedsublattices of the product $(\omega_1+1)\times(\omega_1+1)$, thus showing thatthere are only $\aleph_1$ many of them. In contrast with the last result, weshow that there are $2^{\aleph_1}$ topological types of closed subsets of theTikhonov plank $(\omega_1+1)\times(\omega+1)$.

Paltrow cited irreconcilable differences in the documents, which were filed in Los Angeles on Monday after 11 years of marriage.The papers request joint custody of their two children, Apple and Moses.Paltrow, 42, and Martin, 38, announced their separation in March 2014, describing it as a "conscious uncoupling".The actress's lifestyle blog Goop.com crashed after the couple posted a statement about their decision.They said they had "come to the conclusion that while we love each other very much, we will remain separate".The couple said they had been "working hard for well over a year, some of it together, some of it separated, to see what might have been possible between us"."We are, however, and always will be a family, and in many ways we are closer than we have ever been," they wrote."We are parents first and foremost, to two incredibly wonderful children and we ask for their and our space and privacy to be respected at this difficult time."We have always conducted our relationship privately, and we hope that as we consciously uncouple and co-parent, we will be able to continue in the same manner."Confirmation of the divorce comes afters days of speculation the actress had filed the papers.The documents do not list any details about how the pair will divide their assets, but asks that both sides pay their own lawyers' fees.Paltrow won an Oscar for her role in Shakespeare in Love, while Martin and his band have won six Grammy Awards.They married in secret ceremony in December 2003, days after they announced Paltrow was pregnant with their first child.They had already been together for a year, after reportedly meeting backstage at a Coldplay concert.

Portsmouth City Council has agreed to reassess plans to repaint the Spinnaker Tower red and white following a petition signed by over 5,000 residents. The petition, started by local resident Alex Judd, argued that the proposed color scheme would be inappropriate for the tower, which is a prominent landmark in the city. The council had previously announced a deal with airline Emirates to sponsor the tower, which would have involved painting it red and white, the colors of nearby Southampton's football club. However, the council has now promised to review the plans and consider alternative options.The council's leader, Donna Jones, stated that the authority is committed to maximizing the advertising potential of its assets, but also recognizes the importance of respecting the city's heritage and culture. Jones added that the council would work with the community to find a solution that is acceptable to all parties involved.The Spinnaker Tower, which stands at 560 feet tall, is owned by the council but run by a private attractions management company. The tower has been a popular tourist destination since its opening in 2005 and is known for its distinctive design and panoramic views of the city.The council's decision to review the plans comes after a public backlash against the proposed red and white color scheme. Many residents have expressed their opposition to the plan, arguing that it would be inappropriate for the tower to be associated with the colors of a rival football club.The petition against the plans has gained widespread support, with over 5,000 signatures in less than 24 hours. The petition's organizer, Alex Judd, has expressed his gratitude to the council for reconsidering the plans and hopes that a compromise can be reached.In the meantime, the council has announced that it will continue to explore other options for sponsorship and branding opportunities for the tower.

The microwave phonon stimulated emission (SE) has been experimentally andnumerically investigated in a nonautonomous microwave acoustic quantumgenerator, called also microwave phonon laser or phaser (see previous worksarXiv:cond-mat/0303188 ; arXiv:cond-mat/0402640 ; arXiv:nlin.CG/0703050)Phenomena of branching and long-time refractority (absence of the reaction onthe external pulses) for deterministic chaotic and regular processes of SE wereobserved in experiments with various levels of electromagnetic pumping. At thepumping level growth, the clearly depined increasing of the number ofcoexisting SE states has been observed both in real physical experiments and incomputer simulations. This confirms the analytical estimations of the branchingdensity in the phase space. The nature of the refractority of SE pulses isclosely connected with the pointed branching and reflects the crises of strangeattractors, i.e. their collisions with unstable periodic components of thehigher branches of SE states in the nonautonomous microwave phonon laser.

We report the detection of periodic (p = 1.96 hours) bursts of extremelybright, 100% circularly polarized, coherent radio emission from the M9 dwarfTVLM 513-46546. Simultaneous photometric monitoring observations haveestablished this periodicity to be the rotation period of the dwarf. Thesebursts, which were not present in previous observations of this target, confirmthat ultracool dwarfs can generate persistent levels of broadband, coherentradio emission, associated with the presence of kG magnetic fields in alarge-scale, stable configuration. Compact sources located at the magneticpolar regions produce highly beamed emission generated by the electroncyclotron maser instability, the same mechanism known to generate planetarycoherent radio emission in our solar system. The narrow beams of radiation passour line of sight as the dwarf rotates, producing the associated periodicbursts. The resulting radio light curves are analogous to the periodic lightcurves associated with pulsar radio emission highlighting TVLM 513-46546 as theprototype of a new class of transient radio source.

We chose a nearby mountain range, known for its beautiful views and its difficult trails. As we walked up the first path, the air was filled with anticipation, accompanied by the sweet smell of pine trees. The day of our adventure arrived, and we set off with our equipment and our adventurous spirit. The path was well marked, and each step took us further into the heart of nature. As we climbed higher, the landscape opened up, revealing a magnificent view of majestic peaks and green valleys. A graceful deer crossed our path, apparently unperturbed by our presence. The sound of rushing water attracted us to a hidden waterfall, which offered a refreshing break. We continued our climb, encountering some animals along the way. The climb became more difficult as we reached steeper terrain, which required careful navigation and teamwork. After several hours, we reached the summit, where we were rewarded with a breathtaking view that made the effort of climbing worth every drop of sweat. Our determination and mutual support made the experience even more memorable. We started our descent, our spirits soaring as we reflected on the incredible adventure we had shared. We found a perfect vantage point to watch the setting sun paint the sky in shades of gold and pink. It was a magical moment that will remain forever in our memories. As night fell, we returned to the base camp, tired but satisfied. A warm fire awaited us, and we shared stories and laughter, cherishing the camaraderie we had forged during our climb. The mountain had tested our physical and mental strength, but it had also given us a sense of accomplishment and a new appreciation for the beauty of nature.

If you don't know the pizza in an area, look for the dirtiest, rundown place you can find, and you'll find the best pizza in town. The thinking behind that is, if the place looks that bad but is still in busines, then there is a reason they're still in business. And in my experiences, it's almost always true. nnFiori's is a dive (in a good way)- the inside looks like it was put together in the '70's, and the staff isn't always the friendliest. Forget about parking- there are three spots on the side of the building and perilious street parking on the hill. The later in the evening you go, the busier the dining room. My suggestion is don't eat here- you're only asking for drama. nnBut then you get home with that steaming pie, and all the parking nonsense, questionable customer service, and wait in line vanishes as you're enveloped in cheesey, meaty goodness. The pizzas are big, there are a ton of toppings, and the sauce is sweet in a good way. My suggestion is try their white pizza, which I think rivals the ones I've had in New York. I get mine with ricotta cheese, mushrooms, and meatballs- I'm drooling just thinking about it. I also suggest you try their italian hoagie- big, lots of meat, good dressing, and toasted in a crunchy way- my favorite in Pittsburgh.

Sarah entered the hospital room, carrying a bouquet of fresh flowers that filled the sterile space with their sweet fragrance. Her friend, Rachel, lay in the bed, a weak smile on her face despite the toll that months of chemotherapy had taken on her body. The room was dimly lit, the only sounds being the soft beeping of machines and the muffled voices of doctors and nurses outside.Rachel's eyes lit up as Sarah approached, and she whispered, "Hey, girl! Come to cheer me up?" Her voice was weak, but it was full of warmth and gratitude.Sarah smiled and set the flowers down on the bedside table, taking a seat in the chair next to the bed. "Of course, I couldn't let you suffer alone," she replied, her voice soft and gentle.Rachel smiled back, her eyes shining with tears. "I'm so glad you're here. I've been feeling so alone."Sarah took Rachel's hand in hers, trying to offer what little comfort she could. "You're never alone, Rach. I'm always here for you."As they sat there, the hospital sounds faded into the background, and all that mattered was the connection between them. Rachel closed her eyes, a peaceful expression on her face, and Sarah couldn't help but feel a pang of sadness. She knew that Rachel was in pain, that she was suffering, but at the same time, she was filled with a sense of joy.Joy that Rachel was still with them, still fighting. Joy that their friendship was stronger than ever. Joy that, even in the darkest of times, there was still hope.Sarah leaned over and hugged Rachel tightly. "I love you, Rach," she whispered.Rachel opened her eyes and smiled. "I love you too, Sarah. More than words can say."As they sat there, holding each other, the pain and happiness juxtaposed in a way that was both heartbreaking and uplifting. The flowers on the bedside table seemed to symbolize the fragility of life and the beauty of friendship, and Sarah knew that she would always be there for Rachel, no matter what the future held.
